2605.08851.title: Geometrically Constrained Stenosis Editing in Coronary Angiography via Entropic Optimal Transport
2605.08851.paper_summary: The scarcity of high-quality imaging data for coronary angiography (CAG) stenosis limits the clinical translation of automated stenosis detection. Synthetic stenosis data provides a practical avenue to augment training sets, improving data quality, diversity, and distributional coverage, and enhancing detection precision and generalization. However, diffusion-based editing commonly relies on soft guidance in a noise-initialized reverse process, offering limited pixel-level precision and structure preservation. We propose the OT-Bridge Editor, which reframes localized editing as a constrained entropic optimal transport (OT) problem and leverages geometric information to steer the generation path, enabling stronger geometric control. Extensive experiments show that our synthesized angiograms consistently improve downstream stenosis detection, yielding substantial relative gains of 27.8% on the public ARCADE benchmark and 23.0% on our multi-center dataset, supported by consistent qualitative results.
2605.08851.paper_summary_zh: 高品質的冠狀動脈造影 (CAG) 狹窄影像數據稀缺，限制了自動狹窄檢測的臨床轉化。合成的狹窄數據提供了一種實用的途徑來增強訓練集，提高數據質量、多樣性和分佈覆蓋，並增強檢測精度和泛化能力。然而，基於擴散的編輯通常依賴於在噪聲初始化的反向過程中進行柔性引導，提供有限的像素級精度和結構保留。我們提出了OT-Bridge編輯器，將局部編輯重新框架為一個受限的熵最優傳輸 (OT) 問題，並利用幾何信息來引導生成路徑，實現更強的幾何控制。大量實驗表明，我們合成的血管造影圖在下游狹窄檢測中持續改善，公共ARCADE基準上獲得了27.8%的顯著相對增益，而在我們的多中心數據集中則達到23.0%，並且有一致的定性結果支持。
2605.08851.author: Jialin Li et.al.
2605.08851.authors: Jialin Li, Zhuo Zhang, Yue Cao, Guipeng Lan, Jiabao Wen, Shuai Xiao, Jiachen Yang
